user.title: Battery Badger server
user.description: Connect this Home Assistant to your Battery Badger server. Enter the server URL (e.g. `https://batterybadger.com`) and a long-lived API token generated at `/account/tokens` on the server. Home Assistant will call the server immediately to verify the token and list the installations on your account; the token is stored in Home Assistant's encrypted config entry and only sent back to this same server.

installation.title: Pick installation
installation.description: A Battery Badger account can own more than one installation — one per home, or a dedicated one for testing. Each Home Assistant config entry drives exactly one installation, so pick the one this Home Assistant should manage. Every reading posted from here, and every mode change applied to your inverter, will belong to the chosen installation. You can add another config entry later to drive a different installation from the same Home Assistant.

entities.title: Home Assistant entities
entities.description: Battery Badger needs read access to a few telemetry entities and write access to an inverter control entity. Every half hour the plugin posts a reading (state of charge, household usage in Wh, solar generation in Wh) and fetches a 12-hour schedule; on each `:00` / `:30` boundary it calls `select_option` on the control entity with `CHARGE`, `HOLD` or `DISCHARGE`. Pick a **state-of-charge sensor** (unit `%`, `device_class: battery`), one or more **cumulative energy sensors** for household consumption and solar generation (kWh or Wh — multiple entries are summed), and a `select` or `input_select` with `CHARGE` / `HOLD` / `DISCHARGE` as options. Defaults for SOC and energy are pre-filled from your Energy dashboard where possible; you can edit them later under *Configure*.

data.consumption_entities: Household consumption (cumulative kWh; multiple allowed, summed)
data.solar_entities: Solar generation (cumulative kWh; multiple allowed, summed)

error.cannot_connect: Can't reach the server. Check the URL and that Battery Badger is running.
error.no_installations: This account has no installations yet. Create one on the web dashboard first.

abort.already_configured: This installation is already configured in Home Assistant.

init.title: Edit entity selections
init.description: Re-pick the entities this integration reads from and drives. Defaults show what's currently stored in the config entry. The server URL and API token aren't editable here — remove the config entry and re-add if you need to change either.
